general:
This page allows you to enter the information for creating your publication.

On this page, you can:

  • Click on next to the item you want to enter or edit a piece of information,
  • Type into the input box and then click the icon to save,
  • Click the Preview button above to see how this page will look to the public with your changes so far, or
  • Click on the Submit for Review button above to submit the changes to your draft. A message will pop up with missing required and suggested fields.

title.label: Title
title.general:
The title should be written in plain language and include sufficient detail to facilitate search and discovery.

If this publication can be considered a supplement to a literature publication, consider using the same title with an extra annotation, like ': Supplementary Data'.
title.edit: Type the title into the input box and then click the icon to save.

authors.label: Authors
authors.general: This section should list all authors or collaborators whose work falls under one of the 14 roles listed in the Credit Contributor Roles Taxonomy. All NIST researchers are required to include an ORCID.
authors.list: Click to add authors. Click to make edits.
authors.edit:
Click to save changes.

[Helpful examples, links to policy and guidance]
authors.dragdrop: To change the order of authors listed, click on on the left of each name, drag it to the right place in the list, and release the mouse button to drop it in place.

contactPoint.label: Contact Point
contactPoint.general: Review the name of the NIST contact listed who will respond to questions about this resource. To edit this contact, click
contactPoint.edit:
Click to save changes.

[Helpful examples, links to policy and guideance]

landingPage.label: Visit Home Page
landingPage.general:
The Home Page is the user's entry point for a resource. It provides contextual information and access to the resource itself (e.g., download links for data). Importantly, the DOI (digital object identifier) will point to this URL.

If you wish for this NIST Public Data Repository page to be your home page, leave this box blank and click to close the entry field. If you would like to point to another home page enter the URL here.
landingPage.edit:
Enter a working URL for a home page other than this repository landing page. Click to save changes.

[Helpful examples, links to policy and guidance]

doi.label: Identifier
doi.general: For more information on what a DOI is, see the DOI Foundation page.

version.label: Version
version.general: The “version” of your publication is represented by three numbers that increase with each revision. See the MIDAS User Guide.

description.label: Description
description.general: This field is for a human-readable description of your resource that will enable a user to quickly identify whether it is of interest. You may wish to use part of the abstract of a related publication. Do not copy and paste the paper's abstract here if it does not sufficiently describe the data as a standalone object.
description.edit:
Click to save.

[Helpful examples, links to policy and guidance]

topic.label: Research Topics
topic.general: You are in Research Topics section where you can select topics from the NIST Research Taxonomy. Select one or more topics that apply to this resource
topic.add:
Click to select topics for this resource. Click on the arrows to view subtopics. Double-click on a topic to select it for your resource.

[Helpful examples, links to policy and guidance]
topic.edit:
To edit your Research Topics selections, click on . Double click on topics to add them. Click to the right of topics to delete them.

[Helpful examples, links to policy and guidance]

keyword.label: Keywords
keyword.general: Add subject keywords that help both technical and non-technical users discover this resource. Separate the terms with commas. We recommend entering three to five keywords.
keyword.add:
Click to save.

[Helpful examples, links to policy and guidance]
keyword.edit:
Click to edit your keywords or enter additional keywords.

[Helpful examples, links to policy and guidance]

components.label: Access Page
components.general: You are in the Data Access section. Enter URLs for sites with related information on this resource, e.g. a GitHub repository. Each link requires a URL, title, and description ('About this link'). Note: this area is not for links to files. To upload and link files use the 'Files' button below.
components.list: Click to enter URLs to sites with information related to this resource. Click to make edits.
components.add:
Separate each URL with a comma. When all URLS are entered, click to save.

[Helpful examples, links to policy and guidance]
components.edit: When all URLS are entered, click to save.
components.dragdrop: To change the order of URLs listed, click on on the left of each link, drag it to the right place in the list, and release the mouse button to drop it in place.

references.label: References
references.general:
You are in References section where you can add references or citations related to this resource. You may wish to include links to multiple types of resources such as a related publication or a software package.

[Other Helpful examples, links to policy and guidance]
references.list: Click to add the URI of a related resource. Click to make changes to existing References listed here.
references.add:
When all References are entered, click to save.

[Helpful examples, links to policy and guidance]
references.edit:
When all References are entered, click to save.

[Helpful examples, links to policy and guidance]
references.dragdrop:
To change the order of references listed, click on on the left of each reference, drag it to the right place in the list, and release the mouse button to drop it in place.

[Helpful examples, links to policy and guidance]

Revision.label: Revision
Revision.general:
Placeholder for Revision:

Metadata Update: Placeholder for description.
File removal: Placeholder for description.
Data change (minor): Placeholder for description.
Data change (major): Placeholder for description.
New file addition: Placeholder for description.

isPartOf.label: Is part of
isPartOf.edit:
Placeholder for editing instruction. Click to save.

[Helpful examples, links to policy and guidance]

completed: All data has been completed. No more suggestion.
